---
title: "API Reference"
---

## Overview

This page describes the main functions available in the `wc-sim` package.

These functions cover:

- Data wrangling
- Ranking comparison
- Match simulation
- Model validation
- Parameter tuning

---

## `calculate_weighted_rating`

### Description

Builds a weighted rating table by combining FIFA ranking points with historical World Cup titles.

### Arguments

| Argument    | Description                                                                 |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `rank_df`   | DataFrame containing FIFA ranking data. Must include: `rank`, `name`, `points` |
| `titles_df` | DataFrame containing World Cup titles. Must include: `team`, `wc_titles`   |
| `weight`    | Integer value representing how many points each World Cup title adds to the rating |

### Returns

A DataFrame with the following columns:

| Column            |
|-------------------|
| `fifa_rank`       |
| `name`            |
| `points`          |
| `wc_titles`       |
| `weighted_rating` |
| `weighted_rank`   |

### Notes

This function merges the two datasets and computes a new rating using:

```
weighted_rating = points + (wc_titles × weight)
```

---

## `compare_rank_changes`

### Description

Compares the original FIFA rank with the new weighted rank.

### Arguments

| Argument      | Description                                          |
|---------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `df_weighted` | DataFrame returned from `calculate_weighted_rating`  |

### Returns

A DataFrame with:

| Column          |
|-----------------|
| `name`          |
| `fifa_rank`     |
| `weighted_rank` |
| `rank_change`   |

### Notes

```
rank_change = fifa_rank − weighted_rank
```

A positive value means the team improved its position after including historical titles.

---

## `simulate_match`

### Description

Simulates a single match between two teams.

### Arguments

| Argument       | Description                                        |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| `team_a`       | Name of the first team                             |
| `team_b`       | Name of the second team                            |
| `df_weighted`  | DataFrame containing weighted ratings              |
| `random_state` | Optional integer seed for reproducibility          |

### Returns

A string with the name of the winning team.

### Notes

The probability of team A winning is:

```
P(A wins) = rating_A / (rating_A + rating_B)
```

A random number is used to determine the winner.

---

## `simulate_match_n`

### Description

Simulates a match multiple times and returns win counts and percentages.

### Arguments

| Argument       | Description                                        |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------------|
| `team_a`       | Name of the first team                             |
| `team_b`       | Name of the second team                            |
| `df_weighted`  | DataFrame containing weighted ratings              |
| `n`            | Number of simulations                              |
| `random_state` | Optional seed for reproducibility                  |

### Returns

A DataFrame with:

| Column    |
|-----------|
| `team`    |
| `wins`    |
| `win_pct` |

### Notes

This function is useful for estimating probabilities more reliably than a single simulation.

---

## `validate_weighted_rating`

### Description

Evaluates how well the weighted rating predicts match outcomes compared to raw FIFA points.

### Arguments

| Argument      | Description                                                                                      |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `results_df`  | DataFrame containing historical match results. Must include: `home_team`, `away_team`, `home_score`, `away_score` |
| `df_weighted` | DataFrame with FIFA points and weighted ratings                                                  |

### Returns

A DataFrame with:

| Column                |
|-----------------------|
| `metric`              |
| `correct_predictions` |
| `total_matches`       |
| `accuracy_pct`        |

### Notes

The function:

- Filters matches where both teams are known
- Removes draws
- Compares actual winners to predicted winners
- Calculates accuracy for both metrics

---

## `tune_weight`

### Description

Tests multiple title weight values and evaluates their prediction accuracy.

### Arguments

| Argument     | Description                       |
|--------------|-----------------------------------|
| `results_df` | Historical match results          |
| `rank_df`    | FIFA ranking data                 |
| `titles_df`  | World Cup title data              |
| `weights`    | List of weight values to test     |

### Returns

A DataFrame with:

| Column                |
|-----------------------|
| `weight`              |
| `correct_predictions` |
| `total_matches`       |
| `accuracy_pct`        |

### Notes

This function helps answer the question:

> How much should historical World Cup success influence team ratings?

---

## Summary

The `wc-sim` package provides a simple and modular workflow for:

- Building a custom rating system
- Comparing ranking systems
- Simulating match outcomes
- Validating predictions
- Testing model parameters

Each function is designed to be easy to use and combine with others in a full analysis pipeline.
